holdings.note: SpaceX — IPO'd Jun 2026; FMP may not yet cover earnings/dividend dates for a brand-new listing.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

INTC.read: INTC is +12.65% over five days with no dated company catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div, just a momentum move into thin air. With the position stretched and the only event ahead being September triple witching 86 days out, the asymmetry favors lightening into strength rather than chasing; a pullback toward the breakout shelf would be the spot to re-engage if the turnaround thesis is what you're paid for.
INTC.bull: Foundry/turnaround momentum is building and there's no near-term print to derail the tape.
INTC.bear: A 5-day +12.65% move with no catalyst to validate it is exactly the kind of rip that round-trips on the next macro wobble.

NVDA.read: No dated company catalyst on the board for NVDA right now — the next earnings print isn't loaded, and the only thing ahead is Sept triple witching 86 days out, which is a flow event, not a fundamental one. The position is down 4% on the week into no catalyst, which is more tape than thesis; without an event to lean on, there's no defined add or trim window yet. Wait for the next earnings date to populate before sizing a move around it.
NVDA.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro flow event, not company-specific)
NVDA.bull: AI capex cycle still the dominant earnings driver and a 4% pullback with no negative catalyst is just positioning.
NVDA.bear: No near-term dated catalyst to defend the name if AI-spend narrative wobbles or hyperscaler capex guides soften.

ORCL.read: ORCL is down 16% over the past week and -4.6% today with no company-specific dated catalyst on the calendar to anchor a re-rate — that makes this a tape/positioning unwind rather than an event setup. Without a confirmed earnings date or OCI World/cloud capex update in hand, there's no defined window to lean into; the next real test will be the fiscal Q1 print (typically mid-September), which also coincides with triple witching. Wait for either a dated earnings confirmation or a stabilization signal before treating the drawdown as an add zone.
ORCL.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — triple witching (no company catalyst dated; FQ1 print historically lands in this window)
ORCL.bull: AI/OCI backlog narrative intact; a 16% pullback into an eventual cloud-bookings print sets up a reflex bounce if RPO growth holds.
ORCL.bear: No dated catalyst to stop the bleed, capex-intensity concerns are rerating the multiple, and triple-witching flows can amplify the slide.

NOK.read: No company-specific dated catalyst on the board for NOK — the only thing in front of us is triple witching ~86 days out, which is a market-structure event, not a Nokia story. Position is small (174 sh, ~$2.4k), price action is flat (+0.7% / -1.2%), so there's no stretched winner to trim or capitulation low to add into here. Sit tight until Q3 earnings get confirmed or a network-equipment datapoint (Ericsson print, hyperscaler capex) gives the name a reason to move.
NOK.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no NOK-specific catalyst on the calendar)
NOK.bull: Cheap optionality on AI/data-center networking and Infinera integration; dividend payer with a stable balance sheet.
NOK.bear: Mobile Networks margins still under pressure and FX/Q-on-Q lumpiness can hit the print without warning.

NBIS.read: No company-specific dated catalysts on the sheet for NBIS and no position context (cost basis, P&L, or recent price action) to lean on, so there's no clean window to act into. The only thing on the calendar is triple witching ~86 days out, which is a market-structure event, not an NBIS catalyst — relevant only as a potential volatility spike for a high-beta AI infra name. Until a confirmed earnings date or contract/capacity announcement lands, this is a watch, not a trade.
NBIS.bull: NBIS is a leveraged play on AI compute buildout; any capacity, hyperscaler deal, or revenue run-rate update can re-rate it fast.
NBIS.bear: High-beta, narrative-driven name with no near-term dated catalyst means it trades on AI sentiment and can unwind hard on any sector de-rate or vol event like Sept opex.

KO.read: Tiny 5-share KO position is drifting quietly (+0.4% on the day and week) with no dated company catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div in the window provided. With nothing binary in front of it and the only macro marker being triple witching almost three months out, there's no event-driven edge to lean on either way; this is a defensive sleeve doing its job. Wait for an earnings date or ex-div confirmation to define the next actionable window.
KO.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no KO-specific catalyst on calendar)
KO.bull: Low-beta staple compounding quietly with the dividend; no news is fine news for a defensive holding.
KO.bear: Stretched-ish staples multiple plus no near-term catalyst means rotation risk if rates back up or risk-on accelerates.

SPCX.read: SPCX is a fresh June 2026 IPO with no confirmed earnings or dividend dates in the feed yet — there's no dated company catalyst to lean on, so any add/trim framework here is premature. The only thing on the calendar is the Sep 18 triple witching, which can amplify volatility in a thinly-seasoned post-IPO name as index/options flows build, but it's not a fundamental catalyst. Until the first earnings date prints or lockup details surface, this is a watch — let the company put a dated event on the board before sizing decisions.
SPCX.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no company catalyst confirmed yet)
SPCX.bull: Scarce pure-play exposure to a marquee IPO; first earnings print as a public company could re-rate the multiple if execution lands.
SPCX.bear: Post-IPO names trade on flows more than fundamentals — triple witching, lockup expiries, and a still-unknown earnings date can drive sharp drawdowns with no defensible level.

disclaimer: Forward earnings dates are third-party aggregator estimates, not company-confirmed — confirm against the company's IR release (~2 weeks ahead). Dividends, macro prints and witching are scheduled/confirmed. Not investment advice.
